mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of 2017-02-15 indicates ultra-loose monetary conditions
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises fail to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth and next-5-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that portends lower corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 389,502 million and HOUST at 604 thousand as of 2017-01-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS and HOUST contraction, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.5% year-over-year signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum that supports corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO YoY growth > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending power, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.1% as of 2017-02-15 indicates elevated unemployment
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 5.0%, or correlation between UNRATE level and next-60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% YoY) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 1.5%) indicates monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 226.268 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of 2017-02-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite inflation and rate tightening signal, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining more than 10% month-over-month signals a sharp contraction in residential investment demand that portends weakness in construction employment and consumer wealth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: demand shock or housing market correction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following HOUST declines > 10%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors increase risk appetite.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13260.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-10-15
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
